knimeNode.name: DINOS Numeric Subgroup Discovery
knimeNode.shortDescription: Survival subgroup discovery with the DINOS algorithm
knimeNode.fullDescription:
Finds subgroups in the given data using the DINOS algorithm. Subgroup discovery is a data mining technique for extracting easily understandable "IF ELSE" rules with respect to a chosen target variable. Said rules are supposed to be easily understandable for the final user, distinguishable among themselves with the minimum possible amount of overlap, and taking all rules as a whole they should describe and cover as much of the data as possible.
This specific node discovers subgroups in Survival analysis datasets. This is a specific branch of of statistics which uses specific techniques to analyze datasets with a particular kind of heterogeneous data with a measured time as target variable: The dataset contains both rows that have suffered an event of interest while others haven't (in this last case the data is said to be "censored").
Papers describing the algorithm:
TODO: This is just an example for an hyperlink
TODO: Fix the category where the node appears
Fast Training of Support Vector Machines using Sequential Minimal Optimization
s
Define overall settings for the algorithm The column to define as target for the subgroup discovery. If no column of the support types for this mode (Integer or Double) is found, the node refuses to execute or open the dialogue window The column for indicating if the row is censored or not. Must be Integer, String or Boolean. Cannot be the same as the class column Which value of the censoring column use to indicate censoring. If the value is outside the allowed ranges for the column, the node will issue an error on execution. Define the seed for random generation, if in doubt leave the checkbox unmarked to always use a new seed in every execution. Notice that even with the same seed it is not guaranteed that execution results will be identical among rules.
The DINOS algorithm, as implemented in it's DAF library, is composed of several components for different sections of the algorithm which can be easily swapped to obtain different results. Still, for convenience there are default settings which will suffice for most users.
For each available component there are is a full name and the respective key short name of it's flow variableL Method or formulas to get the quality of a subgroup, with quality defined as a numerical value to be maximized that the larger it is shows the subgroup is more different from the rest of the data This uses the same methods for the metrics, but are instead used in the solution generator (see below) Criteria to select the value of the target variable in each subgroup Determine if a candidate subgroup is feasible Feasibility criteria of an individual, which modifies previously defined candidate subgroups as unfeasible until it is marked as feasible Modifies candidate subgroup to a certain criteria after creation Dominance criteria between two candidate subgroups to use in the genetic algorithm, dominance determines which of two subgroups to be compared is the best Redundancy criteria between two candidate subgroups to use in the genetic algorithm, which determines if the subgroups to be compared cover the same instances i.e: their results overlap each other After each algorithm iteration, which filter to apply to determine if any of the newly generated subgroups is better than the ones previously defined as the best An individual is an internal representation of a candidate subgroup, not final ones as presented to the user At the beginning of each iteration, which method to use to create new candidate subgroups Method to use to determine the best candidates among the initial population Crossover method for the genetic algorithm, which creates a new candidate subgroup from a list of other candidates Mutation method for the genetic algorithm, which alters the given candidate based on a certain criteria
The algorithm will make no more iterations if it does the specified amount of evaluations. Due to the iterative cycle of the genetic algorithm where it replaces subgroups previously defined as the best as it finds better ones the higher this number is, the more chances and time the algorithm will have to find better subgroups. However, making it larger will not magically find better subgroups for the given data, there being a limit depending on both the component configuration and the distribution of the data. After each iteration, whether or not to save the results of metrics. Recommended to have it on. Randomization option for the genetic algorithm Probability of using the designated crossover method for the genetic algorithm If the value obtained by the overlap calculator is higher than the threshold, then the two given subgroups are considered viable to be compared for dominance Genetic Algorithm Variable Maximum amount of candidate subgroups to substitute per algorithm trial Variable used to determine by the solution generator if the population have become stable For the genetic algorithm, if a random generated value is smaller than this then the specified mutation method is called For the genetic algorithm, if a random generated value is smaller than this then the specified crossover method is called Select a minimum for the amount of objectives to select in the "Components Tab" If the calculated overlap between two candidates is greater than this then they are considered redundant Genetic Algorithm Variable

ports.inPort: The table from which to perform subgroup discovery
ports.outPort: Description of each instance and which subgroup it belongs
